title: Pulling cross platform images
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

BUG REPORT

**Description**

I'm trying to pull a arm32v7 image on amd64, for later use with QEMU & `qemu-user-static`. I'm pretty sure this worked in previous releases of podman and it does work on vanilla `docker-ce` on Fedora 30.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman pull arm32v7/debian:stretch` on amd64

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Trying to pull docker.io/arm32v7/debian:stretch...
  Image architecture mismatch: image uses "arm", expecting "amd64"
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

A working pull.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.7.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.5
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @vrothberg @mtrmac I believe this is a change in containers/image?

Yes, this is https://github.com/containers/image/pull/752 behaving as intended: with this check, pulling incorrect-architecture images fails early instead of with a more confusing error (`exec user process caused "exec format error"`) on trying to execute the executables form the container.

(Are there any objections in this behavior in principle?)

Short-term, the `--override-arch` flag should allow overriding this check.

Eventually, https://github.com/containers/image/pull/786 should add some concept of supporting multiple variants versions of an architecture. Right now, that would of course still mean rejecting ARM images on non-ARM systems, but it could then be reasonable to teach Podman “ARM images can be run via Qemu” (is Podman involved in that at all?) and have that impacted in this check.
`--overide-arch` is currently only available on Skopeo, should we add this to podman pull?

```
cmd/podman/pull.go:     flags.StringVar(&pullCommand.OverrideArch, "override-arch", "", "use `ARCH` instead of the architecture of the machine for choosing images")
```
… OTOH it’s marked as hidden, I guess it was intended for testing only or something like that.
Ok so fairly easy to turn on, if people want it.

I can confirm that `podman pull --override-arch=arm arm32v7/debian:stretch` works as well as that building/running works as expected (given another `--override-arch`) argument.

I would be quite helpful if this option wasn't hidden :-)
Thanks for checking! @jschlyter, are you interested in creating a PR to make the flag visible?

I ran into a possibly related problem. I have a container image based on i386/centos:6 that I've archived with "podman save". Podman 1.7 won't let me "podman load" the image (Image architecture mismatch: image uses "i386", expecting "amd64") and doesn't like --overide-arch. Downgrading to podman 1.6 gets everything working again.
Yes, I suppose `podman load` should have the same option (it does not exist at all, unlike `podman pull`, where it exists but is not documented).

> I ran into a possibly related problem. I have a container image based on i386/centos:6 that I've archived with "podman save". Podman 1.7 won't let me "podman load" the image (Image architecture mismatch: image uses "i386", expecting "amd64") and doesn't like --overide-arch. Downgrading to podman 1.6 gets everything working again.

I create a dedicated issue for this one.
